prose: Fenofibrate is returned as a negative under an adversarial-negative tier, and the mechanistic reasoning supports why this is the correct non-flag rather than an oversight. The decisive basis is curated mechanism: there is no CTD curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence and no registration as a neuro-AOP stressor [E1], so no evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis for neurodegeneration can be assembled. The ToxCast bioactivity — 4 mechanistic hits including 2 mitochondrial — is exactly the imposter signal the engine deliberately does not act upon; bioactivity is corroboration only and anti-diagnostic here, never a standalone driver [E2]. Two orthogonal strands reinforce the negative: pharmacovigilance shows no parkinsonism signal across 35,322 FAERS reports (ROR 0.9, not elevated) [E3], and structure-based prediction indicates low brain exposure (BBB-negative), a pharmacokinetic reason it is implausible as a central driver [E4]. Together these strands calibrate confidence in the negative call.

citations.detail: No curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence and not a registered neuro-AOP stressor.
Bioactive: 4 mechanistic assay hits (incl. 2 mitochondrial). This is the imposter signal the engine correctly does NOT act on.
Zero parkinsonism signal across 35322 FAERS reports (ROR 0.9, not elevated).
Low brain exposure (BBB-negative): an orthogonal pharmacokinetic reason it cannot be a driver.
